findings.message: The new `ci-target` volume is mounted under the existing `/workspace` bind mount, so Docker will seed it from the bind-mounted `/workspace/target` visible in the checkout, not from the image-layer `/workspace/target` baked by `dev-base-ci`. That leaves the target volume empty on CI and defeats the cache-reuse change the workflow is trying to add.

`ci-target` is declared as a normal Compose-managed volume, so `docker compose` will namespace it as `<project>_ci-target` unless the volume sets `name: ci-target` (or is marked `external: true`). The workflow seeds a literal `ci-target` Docker volume, so the CI container ends up mounting a different empty volume and never sees the pre-populated `/workspace/target` cache.

The workflow seeds a literal Docker volume named `ci-target`, but this Compose declaration will be project-scoped as `<project>_ci-target` unless you set `name: ci-target` (or `external: true`). When `docker compose -f compose.yml -f compose.ci.yml run ...` starts `ci-runner`, it will mount a different empty volume, so the pre-populated `/workspace/target` cache is never used.

`IN-02` requires `bin/sotp track type-signals` itself to skip missing catalogues and exit zero, but the implementation plan for T003 says the user-facing `sotp track type-signals` path must remain strict while only the gate-path invocation becomes lenient. These two artifacts define opposite behavior for the direct command, so implementers cannot satisfy both the spec and plan as written.

`OS-01` declares source-code changes out of scope, but T003 requires changing `apps/cli-composition/src/track/tddd.rs` to implement the review-gate fix. Because this is a combined track where the second ADR necessarily needs a CLI source change, the out-of-scope item currently conflicts with the implementation plan unless it is scoped explicitly to the CI-cache work only.

`S2` narrows T002 to `.github/workflows/` cache settings and `Makefile.toml`, but `observations.md` records the implemented fix as adding a new `compose.ci.yml` and a `docker exec --user 0 ... chown` step in the workflow. The plan artifacts disagree on the allowed T002 surface, so this track is internally inconsistent.

The T002 notes are stale about how the CI target volume is populated and owned. They say the workflow adds a `docker exec --user 0 ci-runner chown ...` step after starting `ci-runner`, and later keep the overlapping bind-mount volume-seeding concern as a PR-deferred risk. The current workflow instead has a pre-start `Seed ci-target volume from image` step that mounts only `ci-target` into `rust-sdd-template-tools:latest` and performs the chown there, which directly addresses that seed ambiguity. As written, the artifact gives reviewers the wrong implementation model and the wrong fallback diagnosis for a slow PR run.
